campaign.title: Real weeks, adaptive rhythm
campaign.strategy_summary: Real weeks, adaptive rhythm: build habits that bend without breaking.

notes: Regenerated from approved July CMO strategy with stricter tracking, caption, and image prompt rules.

posts.audience_tension: People who want better habits but feel failed by rigid streaks, perfect-day planners, and systems that collapse during stressful or inconsistent weeks.
posts.product_truth_anchor: Progress is visible evidence against the selected rhythm; a broken streak is not the only definition of progress.
posts.hook: If your streak breaks, you are not the problem.
posts.caption: The point is not to pretend interruptions never happen. Use this post as a reminder that visible direction can survive a missed day, then visit Innerbloom to see the early rhythm-led approach.

cta.label: Start with a rhythm that fits your life
cta.truthfulness_note: CTA invites exploration or feedback only; it does not claim guaranteed habit outcomes.

posts.hypothesis: Pain/friction posts will produce a higher landing CTA rate than product mechanism posts because the audience problem is easier to recognize than the product solution.
posts.primary_metric: Landing page sessions/page views from UTM-tagged Instagram posts

visible_copy_plan.headline: If your streak breaks, you are not the problem.
visible_copy_plan.supporting_text: Progress should stay visible, even after an imperfect day.

visual_strategy.visual_goal: Show dashboard progress as the proof that direction can remain visible beyond streak language.
visual_strategy.composition_archetype: editorial type-led brand visual
visual_strategy.claim_to_visual_reasoning: Dashboard progress can prove visible direction; it must not imply missed days disappear or that rhythm changes automatically.

posts.audience_tension: People who want better habits but feel failed by rigid streaks, perfect-day planners, and systems that collapse during stressful or inconsistent weeks.
posts.product_truth_anchor: Progress is visible daily and weekly evidence of continued habit construction against the selected rhythm, not a Monday reset.
posts.hook: You do not need to restart every Monday.
posts.caption: Monday resets can feel tidy until real life interrupts again. Innerbloom is exploring habit progress that keeps continuity visible across uneven weeks.

cta.label: Start with a rhythm that fits your life
cta.truthfulness_note: CTA invites exploration or feedback only; it does not claim guaranteed habit outcomes.

posts.hypothesis: Pain/friction posts will produce a higher landing CTA rate than product mechanism posts because the audience problem is easier to recognize than the product solution.

visible_copy_plan.headline: You do not need to restart every Monday.

jobs.generation_prompt: Create a finished 1080x1080 Instagram square for Innerbloom. Exact visible copy: headline 'If your streak breaks, you are not the problem.' and supporting text 'Progress should stay visible, even after an imperfect day.'. Background: warm off-white, soft botanical gradient, subtle grain, premium calm editorial feeling. Composition archetype: editorial type-led brand visual; place the largest headline in the upper third, the visual proof or metaphor in the center, and the supporting line near the bottom. Type hierarchy: headline is dominant and readable on a phone preview, supporting text secondary, logo quiet. Screenshot treatment: use the registered screenshot as exact UI context in a premium phone mockup or crop only if it supports the adjacent message; preserve all UI labels and values. Brand feeling: grounded, humane, early-adopter honest, not motivational hype. Do not invent UI, screens, file IDs, metrics, testimonials, outcomes, dark assets, or product behavior. The image must be directly usable as the final campaign asset, not a web layout or programmatic render.
